title: -Contract No. 32400019 Amendment: Berkeley Community Media for Public Access Television

sections.recommendation:
Adopt a Resolution authorizing the City Manager to amend Contract No. 32400019 with
Berkeley Community Media (“BCM”) for Public, Educational, and Governmental (“PEG”)
access services for an additional $150,000 for a total not to exceed amount of $1,004,130.
sections.fiscal_impacts:
Funding in the amount of $150,000 is available in the Fiscal Year 2026 PEG Access Fund.
Spending for hardware, software and services in future years is subject to Council approval
of the proposed citywide budget and annual appropriations ordinances.
sections.current_situation:
The additional $150,000 for this contract is solely for capital and infrastructure upgrades
at BCM. The additional funds are covered by existing monies in the PEG Fund. PEG
money is an essential tool for funding public access media services that provide a platform
for community expression, civic engagement, and local government transparency. These
funds, collected from cable providers as part of their franchise agreements (as stipulated
in the Digital Infrastructure and Video Competition Act of 2006), ensure that community
members have access to non-commercial media outlets for educational and informational
programming. Without this funding, BCM would be unable to provide the necessary
services and infrastructure for community members to create and distribute local content.
sections.background:
BCM has relied on PEG funding for many years to operate and maintain its studios,
equipment, and training programs, serving as a vital resource for Berkeley community
members. BCM provides the public with the tools and skills needed to produce their own
television and web content, covering a wide range of topics from local politics and
community events to arts and culture. This funding has historically enabled BCM to offer
a public forum for diverse voices and perspectives that may not be featured on commercial
media.

PEG Funding for Berkeley Community Media (BCM) CONSENT CALENDAR
December 2, 2025
ENVIRONMENTAL SUSTAINABILITY AND CLIMATE IMPACTS
The use of digital media platforms and local broadcasting for community information
reduces the need for physical materials and travel to distribute information, contributing to
a more sustainable and environmentally friendly form of communication. This supports
the City’s environmental goals by lowering waste and reducing the carbon footprint
associated with paper and physical document handling.
sections.rationale:
PEG is an essential mechanism for strengthening our community through increasing and
enhancing communication among our community members. By funding BCM, the City
ensures that the community continues to have a dedicated and accessible platform for
sharing information and fostering a sense of local identity and civic participation. The
recommended funding level will allow BCM to sustain its current operations and continue
to provide valuable media services to the public.
sections.alternatives:
There were no alternative actions considered, as the use of PEG funds is specifically
designated for public access media services and BCM is the designated provider for these
services in Berkeley.
CONTACT PERSON
Kevin Fong, Director, Information Technology, (510) 981-6500
sections.attachments:
1: Resolution
Page 2
Page 3 of 3
RESOLUTION NO. ##,###-N.S.
CONTRACT NO. 32400019 AMENDMENT: BERKELEY COMMUNITY MEDIA (“BCM”)
FOR PUBLIC, EDUCATIONAL, AND GOVERNMENTAL (“PEG”) ACCESS SERVICES
WHEREAS, the City of Berkeley is committed to fostering civic engagement, community
expression, and transparency in government through public access media; and
WHEREAS, Berkeley Community Media (“BCM”) has served as the City’s designated
Public, Educational, and Governmental (“PEG”) access provider for many years, offering
a platform for local voices and content; and
WHEREAS, PEG funding, derived from cable television franchise fees, is specifically
designated to support these vital public media services; and
WHEREAS, a contract amendment with BCM will ensure the continuity of their essential
services, including providing studio and equipment access, media training, and
broadcasting of community-produced content and public meetings; and
WHEREAS, BCM’s operations directly align with the City's goals of providing accessible
information and empowering community members; and
WHEREAS, funding in the amount of $150,000 is available in the Fiscal Year 2026 PEG
Access Fund, and spending for software and services in future years is subject to Council
approval of the proposed citywide budget and annual appropriations ordinances.
NOW THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED by the Council of the City of Berkeley that the City
Manager or delegate is hereby authorized to amend Contract No. 32400019 with Berkeley
Community Media for Public, Educational, and Governmental access services for an
additional $150,000 for a total not to exceed amount of $1,004,130 to be funded by the
PEG Access Fund.
